summary: Cycle M (13th overnight-triage fire of UTC 2026-05-23; crosses UTC day boundary at 2026-05-24T00:01:18Z but local Founder date remains 2026-05-23 ~20:01 ET, so journal + commit retain 2026-05-23 prefix per cycles A→L convention) ran heartbeat-only. Both queues absent on disk for 34th consecutive cycle. regen-all clean: ALL CHECKS PASSED, round-trip 0 failures sustained (26s elapsed at 00:02:24Z; heartbeat written 00:02:24Z status=PASS, 4-field schema with no `source` field — confirms regen-all write path per cycle L's dual-write-path model). Four substantive findings beyond pure heartbeat: (1) CADENCE PATTERN — TWO CONSECUTIVE ~5h GAPS. L→M gap is ~4h 54min wall-clock (cycle L commit ba73671f at 19:06:49Z → cycle M open 00:01:18Z UTC = 4h 54min 29s). Matches K→L gap of ~4h 54min. The ~1h pattern that held G→K (5 consecutive) is decisively broken; cycle M provides the second observation supporting an emerging ~5h cadence post-cycle-K. Carry-over #1 (cron-cadence flag, now 18th observation) escalates from 'pattern itself is uncertain' (cycle L) to 'NEW ~5h pattern emerging post-cycle-K; the G→K ~1h phase was the outlier'. Four interpretations narrow: schedule-changed-at-K, ~5h-is-steady-state, event-triggered-at-~5h, manually-invoked-at-~5h — but resolution requires inspecting cron schedule directly (Founder-decision boundary). (2) FOUNDER VISUAL-REGRESSION BLESS IN L→M WINDOW. Commit 442c4d37 chore(visual-regression): bless iter45 baselines (45 pages × 3 = 135 PNGs) at 2026-05-23T19:11:12Z. Founder active ~5 min after cycle L closed. Cycle M opens ~4h 50min after the Founder commit; no commit-race during cycle M's regen-all run. Consistent with marathon-mode visual-regression discipline per memory project_marathon_2026_05_22_outcomes. (3) NEW UNTRACKED DIR. `.claude/state/design-pass-2026-05-22/home-viewport/` appeared in L→M window (not present in cycle L tree state). Pre-existing Founder marathon work — left alone per isolation discipline. Cycle M's untracked-files inventory now totals FOUR items (was 3 for cycles B→L). Likely contains the home-page viewport captures that 442c4d37 blessed (iter45). (4) HEARTBEAT GATE-FAIL ON CHORE SUBJECT extends dual-write-path observation. Cycle M's open heartbeat showed head_sha=442c4d37 source=post-commit-hook status=GATE-FAIL — the chore(visual-regression) commit. Previously GATE-FAIL pattern was observed on Overnight triage, feat, fix subjects (cycles F→L); cycle M extends to chore subjects. Reasonable hypothesis: post-commit-hook writes GATE-FAIL on any non-routine-cron subject (only `cron(routine):` is exempt). Cycle M's post-regen-all heartbeat correctly overwrites GATE-FAIL with PASS (4-field schema, no source) per the model. Three pre-existing untracked Founder files unchanged from cycle L close (overnight-agent/reports/2026-05-23.md + tests/unit/animate.test.js + tests/unit/utils.test.js); fourth (design-pass-2026-05-22/home-viewport/) added in L→M window; all left alone per isolation discipline. Single yellow user-context-gate continues (main-flows.html ~8.91d stale at cycle M, was 8.71d at L — natural drift over ~5h exactly tracks L→M gap). WELLNESS: status=active throughout (no rest triggered in cycle L close → cycle M open). Cycle M's estimated token consumption ~30k well below 100k threshold. Counters: tokens accumulate (continuous-persona) — cycle L 35k + cycle M 30k = 65k cumulative; hours_active discrete-context (0.5h). Metric-integrity attestation: HONEST — empty-inbox cycle honestly recorded; cadence-pattern confirmation is direct evidence (two consecutive ~5h gaps after 5 consecutive ~1h cycles); Founder visual-regression bless commit + new design-pass untracked dir correctly characterized as L→M window activity; chore-subject GATE-FAIL is direct evidence from heartbeat schema observation. Eight carry-over Founder action items preserved from cycle L close (no new items this cycle; cadence observation is intensification of #1 with 18th observation, GATE-FAIL chore-subject is intensification of #7).
founder_action_items: Cron cadence — now 18th consecutive flag, FURTHER ESCALATED at cycle M close. L→M gap (~4h 54min) is the SECOND consecutive ~5h gap (K→L was also ~4h 54min). The ~1h cadence pattern from cycles G→K is decisively broken. Diagnosis escalates from 'pattern itself is uncertain' (cycle L) to 'NEW ~5h pattern emerging post-cycle-K; the G→K ~1h phase was the outlier'. Four interpretations narrow but unresolved: schedule-changed-at-K, ~5h-is-steady-state, event-triggered-at-~5h, manually-invoked-at-~5h. Founder-action remains: edit overnight-triage cron prompt to add Step-0 guard that exits clean if `git log --since='3h'` shows no inbox motion AND last regen-all heartbeat is < 60 min old. With emerging ~5h cadence, the guard's 3h/60min thresholds may need adjustment to ~6h/360min.
A8_performance: Lighthouse 65/100 on 1 page (target 75+) - open .lighthouseci/*.html, fix top failures (carry-over from cycles S→L; dimension score holds due to rolling-window mechanics).

main-flows.html user-context capture ~8.91 days stale at cycle M (was 8.71 at cycle L — natural drift over ~5h, exactly tracks the L→M gap) - run `node scripts/visual-audit/founder-context-capture.mjs` before next ship-close (not blocking; same diagnosis as cycles S→L).
quota-status weekly_cap field still null - no % computation possible (preserved Founder-triage item from cycles R→L; sidecar discipline OK per [quota-status-schema] check).

aggregate-fiq-status D40 parity GATE-FAIL - stale-timestamp >24h hard threshold tripped because FIQ has had zero churn across 34 consecutive empty-inbox cycles (~34h+ elapsed). NOT an aggregator bug. Will trip on every cycle until FIQ activity resumes or remedy lands (4 remedies enumerated in cycle B post-commit observation).

A12_operational - sustained yellow with continued oscillation across rolling-window mechanics. Cycle M ran clean; current dimension score visible in cycle M's regenerated docs/reports/app-health.html.

Heartbeat `status: GATE-FAIL` field semantics - DUAL-WRITE-PATH MODEL EXTENDED at cycle M close. Cycle L's dual-write-path explanation holds: (a) scripts/regen-all.ps1 writes 4-field minimal schema, no `source`, PASS on round-trip green; (b) .husky/post-commit writes 10-field rich schema, `source='post-commit-hook'`, GATE-FAIL on non-routine subjects. CYCLE M EXTENSION: GATE-FAIL pattern previously observed on Overnight triage, feat, fix subjects (cycles F→L) now extends to chore subjects (cycle M pre-run heartbeat for head_sha=442c4d37 chore(visual-regression)). Reasonable hypothesis: post-commit-hook writes GATE-FAIL on any non-`cron(routine):` subject. REFINED Founder-action: inspect `.husky/post-commit` source code to confirm rule + map exact subject patterns triggering GATE-FAIL. Cycle M stops short of inspecting hook source — Founder-decision boundary for governance change.

Wellness token-counter semantics inconsistency (preserved from cycle K/L close). Tokens accumulate across cron fires (continuous-persona); hours_active does NOT accumulate (discrete-context per cycle F discipline). Cycle M continues honoring this convention. Founder-action: decide whether (a) both reset per cron fire or (b) both accumulate.
